app.name: Probeta DS
app.tagline: Honest data science, end to end.

start.title: Start your experiment
start.subtitle: Upload a CSV or pick an example. Everything runs in your browser; your data never leaves it.

dropzone.label: Drag a CSV here or click to choose

marketing.name: Marketing campaign
marketing.desc: Who converts? Real signal: the model beats the baseline.

rotacion.name: Employee attrition
rotacion.desc: Who leaves? A case where a simple model is enough.

credito.name: Credit default
credito.desc: Ships with a planted leak: watch it get caught.

target.label: What do you want to predict?
target.help: It must be a column with two categories (yes/no, 0/1).

warnings.date: We found columns that look like dates ({cols}). This version doesn't split by time; they're ignored as predictors.

training.title: Training…
training.training: Training and evaluating on test data…
training.wait: The first run takes a few seconds: the engine downloads only once.

results.title: Verdict

verdict.loses: The model does NOT beat the baseline
verdict.beatsDetail: by {delta} in {metric} — {model} vs the best baseline's {baseline}
verdict.tiesDetail: a simple model does just as well: {model} vs {baseline}
verdict.losesDetail: a simple model does better: {model} vs {baseline}. Review your features.
verdict.suspiciousDetail: A perfect result almost always hides a leak, not a great model.

results.testNote: All metrics are computed on the test set, never on the training set.

leakage.title: Possible data leakage
leakage.finding: The column “{column}” could be a proxy for the target.
leakage.hint: Remove it and retrain to see the honest performance.
leakage.none: No obvious leakage found (this check is a help, not a guarantee).

why.title: Why does it predict this way?
why.method: Permutation importance on the test set: how much the model degrades when each variable's relationship with the target is broken.
why.empty: There are no importances to show for this experiment.

narration.title: In words
narration.loading: Generating the narrative and verifying it against the numbers…

fallback.unavailable: AI narration is not configured on this deployment; the standard text is shown.
fallback.provider: The AI provider did not respond; the standard text is shown. Toggle “Narrate with AI” off and on to retry.
fallback.rejected: The AI narrative failed verification against the numbers; for honesty, the standard text is shown. Toggle “Narrate with AI” off and on to retry.

consent.explain: If you turn this on, your column names and aggregate statistics (metrics, importances) are sent to an AI provider. Your data rows are NEVER sent.
consent.verifiedNote: Every narrative is verified against the numbers before display; if it fails, you'll see the standard text.

card.title: Model card
card.desc: The record of your experiment: data, method, metrics, verdict and limits — ready to save or share.

verdict.beats: The model beats the baseline: {model} vs {baseline} in {metric} (an improvement of {delta}).
verdict.ties: The model ties the baseline: {model} vs {baseline} in {metric}; a simple rule does practically as well.
verdict.loses: The model does NOT beat the baseline: {model} vs {baseline} in {metric}; with these variables it adds nothing.

template.features: The variables with the most weight were: {list}.

direction.positive: higher values make the positive class more likely
direction.negative: higher values make the positive class less likely

template.method: Importance was measured by permutation on the test set (data the model never saw during training).
template.leakage: Warning: {columns} could contain the answer (possible leak); these figures may be inflated.

modelcard.title: Model card — {name}
modelcard.generated: Generated on {date} by Probeta DS. All computation happened in the browser: the data never left the device.

sections.explainability: Why does it predict this way?

split.rule: Preprocessing was fitted ONLY on the training set (anti-leakage by construction).

method.pipeline: Imputation (median / most frequent), scaling and one-hot inside a scikit-learn Pipeline.
method.models: Baselines: majority class and logistic regression. Model: Random Forest (200 trees).

leakage.found: Columns suspected of leakage: {columns}. Metrics may be inflated; remove them and retrain.
leakage.none: No obvious leakage found (this check is an honest help, not a guarantee).

explainability.method: Method: permutation importance on the test set (scoring {scoring}, {repeats} repeats).
explainability.notVerified: No AI narrative is included: it was not generated or it failed the numeric verification.

limits.binary: Binary classification only in this version.
limits.leakage: The leakage check catches obvious cases; it is not a guarantee.
limits.explainability: Permutation importance is global (not per-prediction) and can spread across correlated variables; the direction is univariate and does not capture interactions.
limits.dates: Date columns are detected but not used yet (no temporal split).

errors.title: Something doesn't add up
errors.csv-empty: The file is empty or has only a header.
errors.csv-too-large: The file exceeds the 5 MB limit.
errors.csv-ragged: Some rows have a different number of columns than the header.
errors.target-not-binary: The target must have exactly two categories.
errors.no-features: There are no usable columns as predictors.
errors.runtime: Something went wrong while processing. Please try again.
